_description: Content pillar rotation system. Ensures natural content variety across posts. Read by /social to determine today's content focus.

technical_insight.label: Technical Insight
technical_insight.description: Deep-ish observations from actually using AI/LLM tools. Model behavior quirks, performance findings, architecture patterns, debugging stories.
examples: context window limits in practice
model comparison from real usage
agent architecture tradeoff I hit
fine-tuning result that surprised me

tool_workflow.label: Tool & Workflow
tool_workflow.description: Practical tips, CLI tricks, setup guides, tool comparisons. The 'here's how I actually do X' content.
examples: claude code workflow tip
tmux + AI coding setup
prompt pattern that saves time
open-weight model local setup

building_in_public.label: Building in Public
building_in_public.description: What you're working on, what broke, what shipped. Raw and honest — not polished announcements.
examples: shipped a thing, here's what it does
hit a wall with X, tried Y
refactored the agent loop, before/after
this bug took 3 hours to find

industry_reaction.label: Industry Reaction
industry_reaction.description: Hot takes on AI news, model releases, benchmark drama, industry moves. Your genuine opinion as someone who uses these tools.
examples: new model dropped, here's my take
everyone's hyped about X but Y matters more
this benchmark means nothing in practice
the open vs closed debate is missing the point

community_engagement.label: Community Engagement
community_engagement.description: Questions to your audience, polls, 'what's your setup', celebrating other people's work. The 80/20 rule — this is the 80% that's not about you.
examples: what model are you using for X?
this project is underrated
genuinely curious how people handle Y
shoutout to Z for shipping this

_rotation_note: Cycle through this array. Index = total posts made mod array length. Produces natural 40/20/15/15/10 ratio. Read rotation_index from learnings.json to track position.
